commands.review_summary:
The PR changes node pairing so metadata-only refreshes reuse a pending request while caps, commands, or permissions changes create a replacement request, threads permissions through gateway paths, broadcasts superseded pending requests, updates generated Swift models, and adds regression coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main passes canRefreshSingle: () => true in requestNodePairing, so changed caps, commands, or permissions can refresh the same pending request id. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Protected maintainer label plus missing external real behavior proof make this human-handled before merge; the concrete code/doc blockers are the invalid test client id and stale pairing docs.
